https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
RANKIN COUNTY – A narcotics investigation by the Rankin County Sheriff’s Department has resulted in the arrest of a man accused of selling methamphetamine near a church.

Investigators with the sheriff’s department narcotics unit arrested Gavin Lamon Walker on a charge of sale of a controlled substance within 1,500 feet of a church, according to the department.

Officials said Walker’s arrest stems from the sale of methamphetamine in multiple quantities. The investigation remains ongoing and additional charges or arrests are possible.

After his arrest, Walker was taken to the Rankin County Adult Detention Center. The sheriff’s department said he made an initial appearance this week before Rankin County Court Judge David Morrow Jr., who set bond at $50,000.

“It always has been and always will be my priority to keep Rankin County safe from those that would poison it with drugs,” Sheriff Bryan Bailey said in a statement.

The sheriff’s department also thanked the Pearl Police Department for assisting in the investigation and arrest.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: RANKIN COUNTY – A narcotics investigation by the Rankin County Sheriff’s Department has resulted in the arrest of a man accused of selling methamphetamine near a church.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Rankin County narcotics unit arrests man accused of selling meth near church - Darkhorse Press

COVINGTON COUNTY — The Mississippi Bureau of Investigation has issued a Silver Alert for a missing Covington County man believed to be at risk.

According to MBI, 31-year-old Cornelius Elijah Allen of Collins was last seen around 2:39 a.m. Wednesday, Feb. 25, in the 200 block of Monroe Road in Covington County. He was driving in an unknown direction.

Allen is described as 5 feet 9 inches tall, weighing approximately 170 pounds, with black hair and brown eyes. He was last seen wearing blue jeans, a blue shirt and a black hoodie.

Investigators believe Allen may be traveling in a 2017 black Volkswagen Tiguan bearing Mississippi tag 910 CVB.

Family members told authorities that Allen suffers from a medical condition that may impair his judgment.

Anyone who sees Allen or has information about his whereabouts is asked to contact the Covington County Sheriff’s Office at 601-765-8281 or call 911.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: COVINGTON COUNTY — The Mississippi Bureau of Investigation has issued a Silver Alert for a missing Covington County man believed to be at risk.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Silver Alert issued for missing Covington County man - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
JACKSON – The G.V. (Sonny) Montgomery VA Medical Center recognized “Thank a Resident Day” and encouraged patients and staff to show appreciation for resident physicians who care for veterans while completing medical training.

In a social media post, the medical center said resident physicians are doctors in training who work long shifts and manage complex cases while providing care with compassion and focus.

The medical center also highlighted the VA’s role in training the nation’s health care workforce. According to the post, the Department of Veterans Affairs is the largest provider of health professions training in the United States, training more than 120,000 health professionals each year, including about 40,000 resident physicians.

The medical center said nearly 70% of U.S. physicians complete part of their training at a VA facility during their careers, meaning many community health care providers have trained in the VA system.

Officials said supporting VA training programs helps not only today’s veterans but also the future of health care nationwide.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON – The G.V. (Sonny) Montgomery VA Medical Center recognized “Thank a Resident Day” and encouraged patients and staff to show appreciation for resident physicians who care for veterans while completing medical training.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: VA medical center marks “Thank a Resident Day,” highlights role of physicians in training - Darkhorse Press

WIGGINS — The Wiggins Police Department is asking for the public’s help in identifying a suspect connected to a recent incident in the city.

Authorities released images of the individual this week in hopes that someone in the community may recognize them and provide information.

Anyone with information about the suspect is urged to contact the Wiggins Police Department at 601-928-5444.

Tips can also be submitted anonymously through Crime Stoppers using an online tip form, according to police.

Officials thanked residents for their continued support in assisting efforts to keep the community safe.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: WIGGINS — The Wiggins Police Department is asking for the public’s help in identifying a suspect connected to a recent incident in the city.

JEFFERSON COUNTY – Two teenagers were killed in a crash in Jefferson County Friday morning, February 28.

According to MHP, the crash happened at 8:52 a.m. on Highway 552 West in Jefferson County.

A 2018 Nissan Altima driven by 18-year-old Kamarion J. Hyder of Port Gibson, MS, and passenger, 19-year-old Dyamond Dee of Natchez, MS, traveled east on Highway 552 West when it left the roadway, and collided with a tree.

Both Kamarion J. Hyder and Dyamond Dee received fatal injuries from the crash and were pronounced dead at the scene.

This crash remains under investigation by the Mississippi Highway Patrol.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JEFFERSON COUNTY – Two teenagers were killed in a crash in Jefferson County Friday morning, February 28.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: 18 and 19-year-old killed in Jefferson County crash - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
CLINTON — A burn ban is now in effect for the City of Clinton and will remain in place through April 1, according to the city.

Under the ban, no burn permits will be issued, and all outdoor fires are strictly prohibited.

The restriction includes the burning of yard debris, brush piles and open burning of any kind.

Officials are also urging residents not to throw cigarettes out of car windows or discard any materials that could ignite dry grass or brush.

City leaders said dry conditions can cause fires to spread quickly and emphasized that public cooperation is critical to protecting homes, property and first responders.

The burn ban will remain in effect until April 1 unless otherwise updated by city officials.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: CLINTON — A burn ban is now in effect for the City of Clinton and will remain in place through April 1, according to the city.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Burn ban issued for City of Clinton through April 1 - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
JACKSON – Capitol Police are accepting applications for the 2026 Spring Citizens Academy for residents of the Capitol Complex Improvement District, according to the Mississippi Department of Public Safety.

Officials said the academy is scheduled for April 20-28 and will meet from 6 p.m. to 8 p.m. Monday through Friday. An additional session is planned for Saturday from 9 a.m. to noon.

Applications are available through the Mississippi Department of Public Safety website, and the deadline to apply is April 15.

The Citizens Academy is designed to give participants an opportunity to engage with law enforcement and learn more about Capitol Police operations. DPS said the seven-day course will include driver education and simulation activities, a K-9 demonstration, forensic crime scene investigation and a ride-along with a Capitol Police officer.

Participants will also receive an overview of Capitol Police operations, including information on the Capitol Complex Improvement District court and its jurisdiction.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON – Capitol Police are accepting applications for the 2026 Spring Citizens Academy for residents of the Capitol Complex Improvement District, according to the Mississippi Department of Public Safety.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Capitol Police accepting applications for 2026 Spring Citizens Academy - Darkhorse Press

GREENVILLE – The Greenville Police Department is asking for the public’s help in locating a 16-year-old runaway juvenile.

According to police, Madison Brady, 16, was last seen leaving her home Saturday evening around 9 p.m. in Greenville. Authorities said it is unknown what she was wearing at the time.

Brady is described as a Black female with a medium complexion, standing 5 feet 5 inches tall and weighing approximately 165 pounds. She has black, medium-length hair and brown eyes.

Police said anyone who knows Brady’s whereabouts is urged to contact the Greenville Police Department at 662-378-1515, extension 1, or Crime Stoppers at 662-378-8477.

The case remains under investigation.

JACKSON – Jackson police are investigating a double homicide after two men were found dead late Sunday night in the 5300 block of Dogwood Trail near Oakridge Road.

According to police, officers with Precinct 1 were dispatched around 10 p.m. March 2 in response to a reported shooting. Upon arrival, officers located two deceased individuals: a black male lying in a ditch and a white male lying in the street nearby.

Authorities identified the victims as 31-year-old Antonio Arnold and 63-year-old Glen McWilliams. Police said next of kin for both men have been notified.

The Jackson Police Department’s Robbery/Homicide Unit and Mobile Crime Unit responded to the scene, along with the Hinds County Coroner’s Office.

The case remains under active investigation. Anyone with information is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON – Jackson police are investigating a double homicide after two men were found dead late Sunday night in the 5300 block of Dogwood Trail near Oakridge Road.

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
LAWRENCE COUNTY – The Lawrence County Board of Supervisors has voted to ban the sale of kratom at stores located in the county outside municipal limits, according to Sheriff Ryan Everett.

In a statement posted Monday, March 2, Everett said the ban applies to stores in unincorporated areas, or outside corporate limits. He noted the Town of New Hebron previously banned kratom.

Everett said businesses will have 30 days from March 2 to remove kratom products from their inventory. He added that kratom products that are synthetic, blended or contain additives must be removed immediately.

The sheriff said store owners will receive written notice of the changes this week.

No additional details about enforcement or penalties were released.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: LAWRENCE COUNTY – The Lawrence County Board of Supervisors has voted to ban the sale of kratom at stores located in the county outside municipal limits, according to Sheriff Ryan Everett.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Lawrence County supervisors vote to ban kratom sales outside city limits - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
JACKSON – A man has been charged with murder after a woman was found shot to death Monday evening in Jackson.

Officers with Precinct 3 responded around 6:30 p.m. March 2 to the corner of Cromwell Street and Rutledge Avenue in reference to a shooting.

When officers arrived, they found the body of a 36-year-old female lying in the front yard of a home. Police said the victim had suffered one gunshot wound and died from her injury. Her identity is being withheld pending notification of next of kin.

Detectives with the Jackson Police Department’s Robbery/Homicide Unit and Mobile Crime Unit responded to the scene, along with the Hinds County Coroner’s Office.

Several individuals were transported to police headquarters for questioning, including 42-year-old Lincoln Thomas.

Thomas was later booked into the city jail and charged with murder. He is awaiting his initial appearance in Municipal Court.

The investigation remains ongoing. Anyone with information is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department at 601-960-1234 or Crime Stoppers at 601-355-TIPS (8477).
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON – A man has been charged with murder after a woman was found shot to death Monday evening in Jackson.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Man charged with murder after woman found shot to death in Jackson yard - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Therese Apel
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
BENTON COUNTY — It was a hot Mississippi day in Michigan City when L.G. Shaw, a 76-year-old farmer and retired trucking company owner, disappeared into thin air.

Shaw has been missing since July 15, 2009, after disappearing from his property in Benton County. He was last seen that morning at his granddaughter’s home, wearing a plaid shirt and khaki pants. Family members say he left after his usual visit and never returned.

According to his family, Shaw kept a consistent daily routine. Each morning he fed his livestock and made rounds to visit family members who lived within a 3-mile radius. He repeated the same routine in the evenings, often stopping back by his granddaughter’s home around dinnertime.

On the morning he vanished, Shaw told his granddaughter he had been unable to locate one of his colts and believed the animal may have been hung up somewhere. He left to check on it but did not come back that evening.

Concerned when he failed to return and did not answer phone calls, his granddaughter went to his home a short distance away. She found his truck parked at the entrance to his pasture, but Shaw was nowhere around the vehicle.

Inside the home, she found his cigarettes and cell phone on his nightstand — items he always carried in the front pocket of his shirt. Family members say Shaw also routinely carried two wallets, one of which contained a large sum of money. Both wallets were missing.

Shaw’s home is located in Benton County but his pasture extends across the state line into Hardeman County, Tennessee. His granddaughter initially attempted to report him missing in Hardeman County, but authorities determined Benton County had jurisdiction because that is where his home is located.

Family members, volunteers and a Tennessee-based search organization canvassed a 7-mile radius around Shaw’s home for seven days. No trace of him was found.

Shaw is described as a Black male who was 76 years old at the time of his disappearance. He was last seen wearing a plaid shirt and khaki pants.

Anyone with information about L.G. Shaw’s disappearance is urged to contact his granddaughter, D’Aunna, at 731-609-9570.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: BENTON COUNTY — It was a hot Mississippi day in Michigan City when L.G. Shaw, a 76-year-old farmer and retired trucking company owner, disappeared into thin air.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: MISSING IN MISSISSIPPI: L.G. Shaw, Benton County - Darkhorse Press

JACKSON – Mississippi Blood Services is reporting an urgent need for several blood types and is encouraging eligible donors to give as soon as possible.

The organization said O-negative, O-positive, B-negative and B-positive blood types are especially needed to help meet current demand.

Mississippi Blood Services operates donation centers in Flowood, Cleveland and Oxford. The Flowood center is located at 115 Tree St., the Cleveland center at 609 North Davis Ave., Suite 101B, and the Oxford center at 2627 West Oxford Loop, Suite C.

Officials said walk-ins are welcome at all locations. Donors are asked to bring a photo ID and are encouraged to eat well and stay hydrated before donating.

Mississippi Blood Services said blood donations are critical to supporting hospitals and patients across the state.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON – Mississippi Blood Services is reporting an urgent need for several blood types and is encouraging eligible donors to give as soon as possible.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Mississippi Blood Services reports urgent need for several blood types - Darkhorse Press

NATCHEZ — Firefighters responded late Saturday night to a fully engulfed mobile home fire on Lower Woodville Road in Adams County.

Adams County Central Dispatch received a 911 call at approximately 11:14 p.m. March 1 reporting a structure fire at 806 Lower Woodville Road. The caller said that a mobile home was on fire.

The Natchez Fire Department responded to the scene, along with additional units. Deputies with the Adams County Sheriff’s Office also responded to assist with scene security and traffic control.

When first responders arrived, they found the mobile home fully engulfed in flames. Firefighters remained on scene working to contain and extinguish the fire and to prevent it from spreading to nearby areas.

Officials said there were no confirmed reports of injuries as of early Sunday.

The cause of the fire remains under investigation.

Residents were asked to avoid the area of Lower Woodville Road while emergency crews worked.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: NATCHEZ — Firefighters responded late Saturday night to a fully engulfed mobile home fire on Lower Woodville Road in Adams County.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Mobile home destroyed by fire in Adams County - Darkhorse Press

NEWTON – A traffic stop for a noise ordinance violation late Friday night led to multiple charges against a Newton man, according to the Newton Police Department.

Police said officers conducted a traffic stop during the late hours of Feb. 28 in the area of Martin Luther King Jr. Drive for a violation of the City of Newton’s noise ordinance.

Following the stop, the driver was identified as Richard Russell. Authorities said Russell has been charged with violating the city’s noise ordinance, driving with a suspended license, no insurance and being a convicted felon in possession of a firearm.

“The Newton Police Department will continue to enforce the city’s ordinances and state laws no matter how big or small they are,” Chief McKenzie Patrick said in a statement.

No additional details were released.

GULF PARK ESTATES — One man has been arrested and another is wanted following a shooting Thursday night in Gulf Park Estates, according to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department.

Sheriff John Ledbetter said deputies responded at approximately 9:30 p.m. Feb. 26 to a reported shooting near North 19th Street and Pickering Forrest Drive.

When deputies arrived, they found 47-year-old Nathan Lee suffering from two gunshot wounds following an altercation with another party. Lee was transported to a local hospital for treatment and is listed in stable condition.

During the investigation, authorities identified two suspects: 46-year-old Christopher Paul Landry and 32-year-old Glen Guy Barton.

Investigators arrested Barton on one count of aggravated assault with a deadly weapon. Landry is wanted on an active warrant for aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, and his whereabouts are unknown.

The case remains under active investigation, and additional charges or arrests may be forthcoming, officials said.

Anyone with information about the incident is urged to contact the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department at 228-769-3063 or Crime Stoppers at 877-787-5898.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: GULF PARK ESTATES — One man has been arrested and another is wanted following a shooting Thursday night in Gulf Park Estates, according to the Jackson County Sheriff’s Department.

OXFORD: Oxford has been nominated for the USA TODAY 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards in the Best Small College Town category, Visit Oxford MS announced Monday.

Voting is open now and will remain open until noon Eastern time on Monday, March 30. Readers may vote once per category, per day.

“We’re thrilled to see Oxford nominated again for the Best Small College Town category,” said Kinney Ferris, executive director of Visit Oxford MS. “This continued recognition of our beloved college town is a celebration of the unique relationship between the town and the University in an environment that blends academics, athletics, arts and culture.”

Nominees in all categories are selected by a panel of experts, including editors from USA TODAY and 10Best.com.

According to Visit Oxford MS, 10Best.com is known for its original and experiential travel content developed by local travel experts and highlights top attractions, activities and restaurants in destinations across the United States and around the world.

Readers can cast their votes for Oxford online through the USA TODAY 10Best website.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: OXFORD: Oxford has been nominated for the USA TODAY 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards in the Best Small College Town category, Visit Oxford MS announced Monday.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Oxford nominated for USA TODAY 10Best Best Small College Town award - Darkhorse Press

PANOLA COUNTY – The Panola County Sheriff’s Office is investigating a double homicide after two people were found dead inside a vehicle early Sunday morning.

According to authorities, deputies were dispatched around 7 a.m. March 1 to a reported one-vehicle motor vehicle collision on River Road in central Panola County.

Upon arrival, deputies discovered Victor Gonzalez, 39, and Serenity Gonzalez, 19, both of Memphis, Tennessee, deceased inside the vehicle. Investigators said both individuals appeared to have died from gunshot wounds.

The sheriff’s office said the homicides are believed to have happened on Feb. 28 or March 1.

The case remains under investigation.

Anyone with information is asked to contact Crime Stoppers of Panola County at 662-209-2011. Officials said all calls are confidential, and tips leading to the identification and arrest of those responsible may be eligible for a reward.

GLUCKSTADT — Law enforcement officers are investigating a reported threat at Germantown Middle School, officials said.

The Gluckstadt Police Department said officers were on campus and working alongside the Madison County Sheriff’s Office as of the latest update.

Authorities said they are coordinating with school administrators and staff to ensure dismissal proceeds safely and without incident. An increased law enforcement presence remains at the school as a precaution while the investigation continues.

Officials emphasized that student and staff safety is their top priority.

No additional details about the nature of the reported threat were immediately released. Authorities said more information will be provided as it becomes available.

RANKIN COUNTY — A registered sex offender who authorities say had absconded was arrested Friday after investigators determined he was living at an unreported address in Rankin County.

Investigators with the Rankin County Sheriff’s Department said they received information Feb. 27 that Phillip Marvin Winter, a registered sex offender, had absconded and was residing at an unknown location within the county.

After further investigation, deputies confirmed Winter was living at a residence in the Plantation Shore subdivision along Highway 49 south of Florence.

Authorities said Winter was originally ordered to register as a sex offender after pleading guilty to statutory rape in 2008. He was later convicted in 2021 of failure to register as a sex offender.

Following his arrest, Winter was transported to the Rankin County Adult Detention Center, where he is being held on a $5,000 bond pending his initial appearance before David Morrow Jr..

No additional information was immediately released.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: RANKIN COUNTY — A registered sex offender who authorities say had absconded was arrested Friday after investigators determined he was living at an unreported address in Rankin County.

Layton is described as 6 feet 1 inch tall, weighing about 230 pounds, with black, balding hair and brown eyes.

MBI said family members reported that Layton suffers from a medical condition that may impair his judgment.

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Therese Apel
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
CAMDEN — Twelve years after 2-year-old Myra Lewis vanished from her family’s yard in Madison County, the case remains open, the reward remains unclaimed and investigators say the answers are still out there.

This week, the Federal Bureau of Investigation renewed its appeal, offering a reward of up to $20,000 for information regarding her whereabouts. A post from the FBI Sunday served as a reminder of the anniversary so many in the Metro area mark each year, even as the missing posters still hang in the Madison County Sheriff’s Department.

“The #FBI is offering a reward of up to $20,000 for information regarding the whereabouts of Myra Lewis, #missing from Camden, Mississippi, since March 1, 2014: https://www.fbi.gov/wanted/kidnap/myra-lewis ,” it states.

Myra was last seen between 10:30 and 11 a.m. on March 1, 2014, playing outside her home in Camden. She was wearing white or khaki pants, a turquoise sweater with a bear on the front and pink tennis shoes. At the time, she was 3 feet, 1 inch tall and weighed 27 pounds, with black hair and brown eyes.

The child’s disappearance is Mississippi’s longest-standing Amber Alert case. Despite massive searches, national attention and years of investigation, no one has been charged and no trace of the toddler has ever been found.

In 2025, Randy Tucker spoke publicly in unprecedented detail on the “Investigation: Homicide” podcast about the day Myra disappeared and the suspicions that have lingered since.

From the beginning, Tucker said, certain elements of the scene troubled investigators, particularly the response of tracking dogs brought to the property.

A tracking dog refused to leave the immediate area of the yard where Myra had been playing, Tucker said. A cadaver dog later alerted to the back of a side-by-side ATV on the property. That was a detail the sheriff described as “very strange,” given there was no clear explanation for Myra’s scent to be there.

Searchers combed the area for days, dragging ponds, searching woods and even examining recent burial sites. Volunteers and law enforcement from local, state and federal agencies participated. Still, Myra was “gone like a breath in the wind,” Tucker said.

SEE MYRA LEWIS in MISSING IN MISSISSIPPI

Over the years, the sheriff has grown increasingly convinced that the truth lies within a very small circle.

“I firmly believe to this very date that the people that were on that property that day know exactly what happened, know exactly where Myra is, and the circumstances surrounding her disappearance,” Tucker said on the podcast.

He pointed to inconsistencies in statements about Myra’s whereabouts in the days before she was reported missing, including conflicting accounts about whether she attended school the day before. He also noted what he described as unusual behavior from family members in the immediate aftermath and in the years since.

Attempts by media, including Darkhorse Press, to contact family members over the years have gone unanswered. Tucker has said he believes someone may eventually come forward.

“Until somebody clears their conscience, we’re no closer than we were,” he said.

The case remains active. Authorities continue to receive occasional tips from across the country, though none have led to a breakthrough.

The FBI urges anyone with information about Myra Lewis to contact their local FBI office, the nearest American Embassy or Consulate, or submit an anonymous tip online. Officials say the $20,000 reward remains available for information leading to her whereabouts.

Twelve years later, the smiling toddler in pink remains frozen in time on missing posters, and investigators say they are still waiting for the piece of the puzzle that completes the picture of what happened to little Myra.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: CAMDEN — Twelve years after 2-year-old Myra Lewis vanished from her family’s yard in Madison County, the case remains open, the reward remains unclaimed and investigators say the answers are still out there.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Twelve years later: Myra Lewis’ disappearance still haunts Mississippi - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Therese Apel
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
JACKSON — Clinics at the University of Mississippi Medical Center reopened today and resumed normal operations and appointment schedules following a cyberattack that disrupted network systems last month.

The medical center announced that all clinics are back to regular hours as of Monday and will begin rescheduling patient appointments that were canceled after the Feb. 19 attack. Officials said clinics will operate extended hours and additional days in the coming weeks to accommodate as many impacted patients as possible.

“It will take some time to fully recover and to investigate this criminal attack on our network systems. This was not just an attack on UMMC; it was also an attack on our patients, students, faculty and staff,” said Dr. LouAnn Woodward, vice chancellor for health affairs. “Thank you to our patients and their families for their patience as we worked diligently to restore access to the services that they need.”

Patients regained access to their MyChart accounts over the weekend as the electronic health record system came back online Saturday. With phone lines and internet service restored, UMMC has also resumed regular patient call center operations.

The UMMC Triage Line will remain open for a few days this week before calls are transitioned back to the staffed call center. Patients with time-sensitive medical needs can contact the triage line at 601-815-0000.

Hospital officials said recovery efforts are ongoing as they continue investigating the cyberattack and working to fully restore all systems.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON — Clinics at the University of Mississippi Medical Center reopened today and resumed normal operations and appointment schedules following a cyberattack that disrupted network systems last month.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: UMMC clinics reopen today after cyberattack; normal operations resume - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
ADAMS COUNTY – Authorities are investigating a residential fire that occurred Monday evening on Morgantown Road in Natchez.

According to the Adams County Sheriff’s Office, deputies received a 911 call at approximately 7:47 p.m. March 2 reporting a fire at a residence located at 69 Morgantown Road.

Deputies were dispatched at 7:50 p.m. and arrived on scene around 7:54 p.m., where they observed smoke coming from the home. Multiple units responded to assist. The Natchez Fire Department was called to extinguish the blaze, and Entergy was notified as a precaution.

The caller told dispatchers they were unsure whether anyone was inside the home at the time of the fire. Deputies secured the area while firefighters worked to contain and suppress the flames.

The cause of the fire remains under investigation, and authorities are working to determine the extent of the damage. No injuries had been confirmed as of Monday night.

Anyone with information regarding the incident is urged to contact the Adams County Sheriff’s Office.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: ADAMS COUNTY – Authorities are investigating a residential fire that occurred Monday evening on Morgantown Road in Natchez.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Adams County deputies, Natchez firefighters respond to Morgantown Road house fire - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
GREENVILLE – The Greenville Fire Department has started its annual fire hydrant painting program, and residents may soon notice more bright red hydrants appearing throughout the city.

Fire officials said the painting is more than a cosmetic update. The vivid red color helps firefighters quickly spot working hydrants, allowing crews to make faster connections and secure a reliable water supply during emergencies.

The department said the proactive effort is aimed at improving response times and strengthening public safety.

The Greenville Fire Department thanked the community for its continued support.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: GREENVILLE – The Greenville Fire Department has started its annual fire hydrant painting program, and residents may soon notice more bright red hydrants appearing throughout the city.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Greenville Fire Department Begins Annual Hydrant Painting Across City - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
INDIANOLA — A 22-year-old man wanted in connection with a February shooting that seriously injured a woman and her 7-year-old grandson has been arrested following a fugitive investigation by the U.S. Marshals Service.

Delvin Jones was taken into custody March 2 at a residence in the 100 block of Jordan Drive in Indianola. Jones was wanted by the Indianola Police Department on a charge of aggravated assault with a deadly weapon likely to produce death or serious bodily harm.

According to authorities, the shooting occurred Feb. 12 on West Percy Street in Indianola. A 46-year-old woman and her 7-year-old grandson were shot and seriously injured after their vehicle was riddled with bullets. A 2-year-old child who was also inside the vehicle was not injured.

A warrant was issued for Jones’ arrest Feb. 19. Investigators said the motive for the shooting remains under investigation.

Deputy U.S. marshals assigned to the Gulf Coast Regional Fugitive Task Force tracked Jones to the Jordan Drive address. With assistance from the Indianola Police Department, law enforcement surrounded the residence and took Jones into custody without incident.

Officials said a Glock pistol was recovered during the arrest and seized as possible evidence.

The Gulf Coast Regional Fugitive Task Force, established in 2006, partners with more than 60 federal, state and local agencies and has apprehended more than 70,000 fugitives since its inception.

Anyone with information about wanted fugitives is urged to contact the nearest U.S. Marshals office, the U.S. Marshals Service Communications Center at 1-800-336-0102, or submit a tip online through the agency’s website.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: INDIANOLA — A 22-year-old man wanted in connection with a February shooting that seriously injured a woman and her 7-year-old grandson has been arrested following a fugitive investigation by the U.S. Marshals Service.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Indianola man arrested by Marshals in shooting of grandmother, 7-year-old - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
LOWNDES COUNTY – A social media post showing a trailer being stolen from a residence led to an arrest and the recovery of the property, according to the Lowndes County Sheriff’s Office.

Deputies said the video circulated online and helped investigators identify the suspect as Michael Paul Hughes, 58. Authorities said deputies later located Hughes, took him into custody and recovered the stolen trailer.

Sheriff Eddie Hawkins credited community awareness and quick reporting for helping bring the case to a swift resolution.

“If you see suspicious activity in your area, please report it immediately,” Hawkins said. “Working together, we can keep our community safe.”

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Lowndes County Sheriff’s Office at 662-328-6788 or Golden Triangle Crime Stoppers at 1-800-530-7151, or submit tips through the P3 Tips app. Crime Stoppers tips can be made anonymously, and callers may be eligible for a cash reward if information leads to an arrest.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: LOWNDES COUNTY – A social media post showing a trailer being stolen from a residence led to an arrest and the recovery of the property, according to the Lowndes County Sheriff’s Office.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Lowndes County deputies recover stolen trailer, arrest suspect - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Cash Matlock
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
LAUDERDALE COUNTY – A Meridian man faces multiple felony charges after authorities executed a search warrant at a local business Friday as part of an ongoing narcotics investigation.

The East Mississippi Drug Task Force, with assistance from the Lauderdale County SWAT Team, conducted the operation Feb. 27 at a business in the 3600 block of 8th Street in the City of Meridian.

During the search, agents reported seizing more than two ounces of methamphetamine, a large amount of U.S. currency and two firearms. Investigators also recovered several items believed to be stolen and possibly connected to other open investigations.

Authorities said multiple illegal gambling machines were discovered inside the business. The Mississippi Gaming Commission was notified and responded to assist with the seizure and destruction of the devices.

Samuel Allen was arrested and charged with two counts of sale of methamphetamine within 1,500 feet of a church (X2), with bond set at $75,000. He is also charged with trafficking methamphetamine within 1,500 feet of a church while in possession of a firearm, with bond set at $350,000, and felon in possession of a firearm, with bond set at $250,000.

Officials said the arrest stems from an ongoing investigation into illegal drug sales in the community. Authorities encourage anyone with information related to the case to contact the Lauderdale County Sheriff’s Department through its website or call Crime Stoppers at 855-485-8477.

The investigation remains active, and additional charges are expected. All individuals are presumed innocent unless proven guilty in a court of law.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: LAUDERDALE COUNTY – A Meridian man faces multiple felony charges after authorities executed a search warrant at a local business Friday as part of an ongoing narcotics investigation.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Man Arrested After Drug Task Force Raid on Meridian Business - Darkhorse Press

TUPELO — Tupelo police are investigating a shooting that occurred Tuesday evening at a gas station on West Jackson Street.

According to the Tupelo Police Department, officers responded to a report of a shooting at the West Jackson Mart, located at 1391 W. Jackson St., at the corner of West Jackson Street and Lumpkin Avenue.

Police said one victim was transported to North Mississippi Medical Center with non-life-threatening injuries.

Officers remained on scene as of Tuesday night and were actively investigating the incident. Authorities said there is no known threat to the public at this time and believe the shooting to be an isolated incident.

Additional information will be released as it becomes available.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: TUPELO — Tupelo police are investigating a shooting that occurred Tuesday evening at a gas station on West Jackson Street.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: One injured in shooting at West Jackson Mart in Tupelo - Darkhorse Press

PASCAGOULA – The Pascagoula Police Department is asking for the public’s help in locating a suspect wanted in connection with a felony shoplifting investigation.

Police said Carldarone Williams is suspected of stealing 10 pairs of eyeglasses from the Walmart Vision Center on Feb. 20. Authorities estimate the total value of the items at more than $2,100.

Investigators are requesting assistance from anyone who may know Williams’ whereabouts.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Pascagoula Police Department at 228-762-2211 or send a message through the department’s official social media page.

PEARL RIVER COUNTY – Authorities are asking for the public’s assistance in locating a missing 16-year-old who left her residence early Tuesday morning.

According to officials, Starlyn McIntyre left her home at approximately 2 a.m. in her family’s 2013 Chrysler Town and Country van. The vehicle bears Mississippi tag DB4768U.

It is currently unknown where McIntyre may be headed.

Anyone with information regarding her whereabouts is urged to contact Pearl River County Central Dispatch at 601-749-5482 or send a message through the department’s official social media platform.

The case remains active.

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
JACKSON — A pedestrian traveling in a wheelchair was killed Monday evening after being struck by two vehicles on Raymond Road, according to the Jackson Police Department.

Officers responded about 6:40 p.m. to the intersection of Raymond Road and Wilson Boulevard for a reported fatal crash. Police said the pedestrian was hit by a 2012 white Ford Fusion and a 2010 silver Chevrolet Malibu.

According to investigators, the pedestrian was traveling eastbound on Raymond Road in a wheelchair near the center lane when he was struck by the Ford Fusion, which was also traveling east. The impact ejected the victim into the westbound lanes, where he was then struck by the Chevrolet Malibu traveling west in the inside lane.

The victim became trapped underneath the Malibu and was extricated by the Jackson Fire Department. He was pronounced dead at the scene.

Both drivers remained at the scene and were interviewed by responding officers and an accident investigator. Police said each driver was issued a citation for no insurance.

The investigation is ongoing.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON — A pedestrian traveling in a wheelchair was killed Monday evening after being struck by two vehicles on Raymond Road, according to the Jackson Police Department.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Pedestrian in Wheelchair Killed in Two-Vehicle Crash on Raymond Road - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
GULFPORT – A Gulfport man has been arrested and charged after two people were injured in a reported cutting Monday night on Palmer Drive, according to the Gulfport Police Department.

Police said 35-year-old Shaun Andrew Lawler of Gulfport was arrested March 2 and charged with one count of aggravated domestic assault and one count of aggravated assault.

Officers responded around 9:40 p.m. to a report of a cutting in the 2800 block of Palmer Drive. When officers arrived, they found two victims suffering from multiple lacerations, police said. Both victims were transported to a local hospital and were listed in stable condition.

Lawler was located a short time later and taken into custody.

Police said the investigation indicated the incident stemmed from Lawler reportedly being awake for several days due to the use of narcotics.

Lawler was processed and transported to the Harrison County Adult Detention Center, where he is being held on a $400,000 bond.

Anyone with information is asked to contact the Gulfport Police Department at 228-868-5959 or Mississippi Coast Crime Stoppers at 877-787-5898.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: GULFPORT – A Gulfport man has been arrested and charged after two people were injured in a reported cutting Monday night on Palmer Drive, according to the Gulfport Police Department.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Police say narcotics kept suspect awake for days before Gulfport attack - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Therese Apel
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
RIDGELAND — The Ridgeland Police Department has kicked off its annual “Cover the Cruiser” fundraiser to benefit Special Olympics Mississippi, inviting residents to donate and sign their names on a patrol vehicle in support of local athletes.

The fundraiser runs through May 1 at participating locations around the city. Community members can stop by, make a donation of any amount and add their signature to a marked patrol car as part of the campaign. All proceeds go directly to programs that help Special Olympics athletes train, compete and succeed.

“No donation is too small — and every signature makes a difference,” the department said in announcing the event, encouraging residents to show their support for athletes across the state.

According to Special Olympics’ website, the organization is a global sports movement that works to unleash the human spirit through the transformative power and joy of sport. Through programming in sports, health, education and community building, Special Olympics addresses inactivity, stigma, isolation and injustice faced by people with intellectual disabilities.

The organization provides high-quality training and competition in an inclusive culture through Unified Sports, which allows individuals with and without intellectual disabilities to compete together. Special Olympics offers more than 30 Olympic-style individual and team sports for athletes ages 2 to 99. Programs also include Unified Schools initiatives and Young Athletes, which helps children as young as 2 develop basic skills.

The Ridgeland Police Department’s fundraiser is part of ongoing efforts by law enforcement agencies nationwide to support Special Olympics through community-based events.

Residents can watch for the marked patrol vehicle at locations throughout Ridgeland through May 1 to participate.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: RIDGELAND — The Ridgeland Police Department has kicked off its annual “Cover the Cruiser” fundraiser to benefit Special Olympics Mississippi, inviting residents to donate and sign their names on a patrol vehicle in support of local athletes.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Ridgeland Police Launch ‘Cover the Cruiser’ Fundraiser for Special Olympics Mississippi - Darkhorse Press

JACKSON — The Mississippi Bureau of Investigation has activated a Silver Alert for a 63-year-old Jackson man who was last seen Thursday morning.

Greg King was last seen about 10:30 a.m. Feb. 26 in the 1600 block of Westhaven Boulevard in Jackson, according to the Mississippi Bureau of Investigation. Authorities said he was walking in an unknown direction at the time.

King is 5 feet 5 inches tall, weighing approximately 140 pounds, with brown hair and blue eyes.

Family members told authorities that King suffers from a medical condition that may impair his judgment.

The Silver Alert was issued on behalf of the Jackson Police Department.

Anyone with information about King’s whereabouts is asked to contact the Jackson Police Department or local law enforcement immediately.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON — The Mississippi Bureau of Investigation has activated a Silver Alert for a 63-year-old Jackson man who was last seen Thursday morning.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Silver Alert issued for 63-year-old Jackson man - Darkhorse Press

JACKSON – Authorities have issued a Silver Alert for a 35-year-old man reported missing from a personal care home in Jackson.

Larry Layton, 35, was last seen at St. David’s Personal Care Home in the 3000 block of Capitol Street. Officials said he walked away from the facility.

Layton is described as 6 feet 1 inch tall and weighing approximately 230 pounds. His clothing description at the time of his disappearance is unknown.

Authorities said Layton has been diagnosed with paranoid schizophrenia.

Anyone with information on Layton’s whereabouts is urged to contact local law enforcement immediately.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON – Authorities have issued a Silver Alert for a 35-year-old man reported missing from a personal care home in Jackson.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: Silver Alert issued for missing 35-year-old man with paranoid schizophrenia in Jackson - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Morgan Howard
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
CLINTON — What felt like Christmas in March arrived this week at Community Animal Rescue & Adoption.

Members of the Baptist Healthplex in Clinton organized a donation drive to support the animals at CARA, delivering a large collection of supplies for the shelter’s dogs and cats.

Shelter officials said the outpouring of generosity left staff and volunteers overwhelmed with gratitude. While a photo captured a portion of the donated items, CARA representatives said it did not reflect the full scope of what was given.

“We are absolutely blown away by their generosity,” CARA said in a statement. “There is no way to convey how incredible it feels to know how much our community cares and supports our mission and the animals we care for here.”

CARA expressed appreciation for the Healthplex members who contributed, calling the effort humbling and deeply meaningful.

The shelter relies heavily on community support to provide food, medical care and safe housing for animals awaiting adoption. Officials said acts of kindness like this help ensure the organization can continue its mission of rescuing, rehabilitating and rehoming animals in need.

CARA extended a heartfelt thank-you to everyone who participated in the drive, noting that the generosity will make a direct impact on the lives of the animals in its care.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: CLINTON — What felt like Christmas in March arrived this week at Community Animal Rescue & Adoption.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: STEPPING UP TO SERVE: Baptist Healthplex Members Give Generous Donation to CARA - Darkhorse Press

https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.author: Therese Apel
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.content_html:
JACKSON — Burn bans are now in effect in at least 15 Mississippi counties as dry conditions increase wildfire risk, according to the Mississippi Forestry Commission.

The bans prohibit outdoor burning of any kind and will remain in place through various dates in March and early April. County boards of supervisors request burn bans, and the Forestry Commission approves them. All burn bans expire at midnight on the listed end date.

Counties currently under burn bans include:

A burn ban means no outdoor burning of any kind. Anything with an open flame that produces an ember is prohibited, including campfires, bonfires, fire pits, fire rings, burn barrels, debris burning and field burning. Officials warn that wind can carry embers up to a half-mile away, potentially igniting spot fires.

Allowed during a burn ban are propane or gas grills and heaters, as well as charcoal grills, if used according to manufacturer guidelines, away from combustible materials and never left unattended. Officials stress that charcoal briquettes should be completely cooled and doused with water before disposal in a metal container, with ash cold to the touch.

Each burn ban lists whether exemptions apply. Exemptions may include certified burn managers, county fire services, certain commercial contractors with heavy construction equipment that meet Mississippi Department of Environmental Quality regulations, and agricultural field burns. Some counties list no exemptions.

Local sheriff’s departments enforce burn bans. Anyone who knowingly and willfully violates a burn ban is guilty of a misdemeanor and may face a fine ranging from $100 to $500.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.summary: JACKSON — Burn bans are now in effect in at least 15 Mississippi counties as dry conditions increase wildfire risk, according to the Mississippi Forestry Commission.
https://darkhorsepressnow.com/news/2026-.title: UPDATE: Burn Bans Issued in at least 15 Mississippi Counties; Violations Carry Fines - Darkhorse Press
